title: BMad HITL Evaluation Schema
description: Schema for HITL (Human-In-The-Loop) evaluation of agents and skills. Defines the structure for test scenarios that simulate multi-turn conversations with diverse user personas.

name.description: Name of the agent or skill being tested

description.description: Description of the evaluation suite and what it tests

version.description: Version of the eval suite (semantic versioning recommended)

id.description: Unique identifier for the scenario (kebab-case, used in directory names)

description.description: What this scenario tests and why it matters

persona.description: Detailed description of the user persona being simulated. Include: age/background, communication style, what matters to them, typical response patterns.

goal.description: What the user is trying to accomplish in this interaction

initial_input.description: The user's first message to kick off the conversation

success_criteria.description: Observable outcomes that indicate success. Each criterion should be specific and verifiable from the transcript.

failure_modes.description: What constitutes failure for this scenario. Helps the grader identify anti-patterns.

fixture.description: Optional path to a fixture file (relative to tests/fixtures/) that provides input substrate for the test. Used for conversion, analysis, or continuation scenarios.

known_deficiencies.description: For analysis/judgment scenarios: a list of known issues that should be found. Used for LLM-as-judge validation where the grader checks against expected findings.

context.description: Additional context provided to the UserSimulator or agent for this scenario

existing_agent.description: Content of an existing agent being edited or analyzed

path.description: File path to the existing agent

content.description: Full content of the agent file

examples.name: My Agent
examples.description: HITL evaluation suite for My Agent

evals.name: simple-task-completion
evals.description: User wants to complete a straightforward task
evals.persona: Sam, 30, busy professional. Wants quick results, gives brief answers, gets impatient with unnecessary questions.
success_criteria: Interaction stays focused on the task
User's time is respected (minimal unnecessary conversation)
